behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the file src/bun.js/api/bun/dns.zig and the exact patterns the tests check for: 'std.heap.stackFallback(1024, bun.default_allocator)', 'name_allocator.dupeZ(u8, query.name)', 'defer name_allocator.free(name_z)', and the banned 'var name_buf: [1024]u8 = undefined'.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction dictates the exact literal patterns that must appear (e.g. 'std.heap.stackFallback(1024, bun.default_allocator)', 'name_allocator.dupeZ(u8, query.name)', 'defer name_allocator.free(name_z)'), which essentially spells out the fix including variable name 'name_allocator' and 'name_z'.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests require literal strings like 'defer name_allocator.free(name_z)' and 'name_allocator.dupeZ(u8, query.name)' — a correct alternative fix using different variable names (e.g. 'allocator.dupeZ' or 'defer alloc.free(name)') would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail-to-pass tests are regex text checks on dns.zig (e.g. test_no_fixed_buffer_used, test_stack_fallback_allocator_used, test_dynamic_string_duplication); subprocess calls only run 'zig fmt --check' and 'zig ast-check', not behavioral DNS tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific patterns to be present and absent in the real code (non-comment), so a stub wouldn't pass — though they're text-based, they do require the actual change.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three required patterns from the instruction (stackFallback, dupeZ, defer free) and the banned fixed buffer pattern are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by comment banners into pass-to-pass static, repo_tests, and fail-to-pass sections with descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and patterns in instruction.md match those used in tests and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact file 'src/bun.js/api/bun/dns.zig' and the exact literal patterns to insert, leaving nothing to localize or design.
